title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 6 - A Look at the Core Sets (Part Five)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aoh738yJBRA

Daniel (@dungeondive) wraps up the core sets coverage with a detailed look at standout heroes and enemies from each box.

From Forbidden Fortress, the Assassin gets the spotlight—a nimble, armour-piercing melee specialist who can't use guns but compensates with smoke bomb teleportation, shurikens, and ninja clan affiliations that shape each build differently. From Swamps of Death, it's the Preacher, Daniel's favourite archetype, complete with faith-powered sermons split between blessings and judgments. He reveals plans for a husband-and-wife minister duo using a Reaper Chronoscope proxy for the nun—one wielding the new Redemptionist Shotgun, the other carrying the holy book as support. The Gunslinger from City of the Ancients rounds out the heroes with his special bullet system and devastating quick draw ability.

On the monster side, the Living Statue, Harbinger, and Goliath each get examined alongside their stat cards and new trait pack expansions that add variety to otherwise basic enemies. Daniel recommends picking up the trait packs to inject fresh challenge into the original sets' more straightforward encounters. He notes that he prefers capping campaigns around level four to avoid excessive bookkeeping as elite abilities stack up.

----

Do you build characters around mechanical optimisation, or do you let the narrative shape your choices?
